title: Speech-Language Pathology Assistants
description: Assist speech-language pathologists in the assessment and treatment of speech, language, voice, and fluency disorders. Implement speech and language programs or activities as planned and directed by speech-language pathologists. Monitor the use of alternative communication devices and systems.

tasks.text: Document clients' progress toward meeting established treatment objectives.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Implement treatment plans or protocols as directed by speech-language pathologists.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Collect and compile data to document clients' performance or assess program quality.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform support duties, such as preparing materials, keeping records, maintaining supplies, and scheduling activities.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assist speech-language pathologists in the remediation or development of speech and language skills.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Select or prepare speech-language instructional materials.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assist speech-language pathologists in the conduct of client screenings or assessments of language, voice, fluency, articulation, or hearing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Prepare charts, graphs, or other visual displays to communicate clients' performance information.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Test or maintain equipment to ensure correct performance.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct in-service training sessions, or family and community education programs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assist speech-language pathologists in the conduct of speech-language research projects.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
